habitat: Subtropical/tropical moist montane forests; bamboo thickets; and scrub
story: Discover the stunning Golden Parrotbill (*Suthora verreauxi*), a member of the diverse Paradoxornithidae family. Native to Subtropical/tropical moist montane forests; bamboo thickets; and scrub, this bird faces various environmental challenges. Currently classified as Least Concern (LC) on the IUCN Red List, the Golden Parrotbill represents the delicate balance of its native region. Understanding its habitat requirements and distribution is key to effective conservation planning. This species contributes to the richness of avian diversity, and this poster offers an artistic glimpse into its world.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
